Comment: Helicopter on a RealFlight heli model. DESIGN RULE: this table is a PURE IDENTITY PIPE for all twelve channels - rf N == px4 N, RealFlight channel N+1 is driven by PX4 output channel N+1, with no renumbering and no reversal anywhere. The ordering decision lives entirely in PWM_MAIN_FUNC* (SITL, ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1203_flightaxis_heli) and HIL_ACT_FUNC* (HITL, ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d/airframes/1203_flightaxis_heli.hil), the same way ArduPilot uses SERVOn_FUNCTION and PX4's own gz bridge uses SIM_GZ_*_FUNC*. Do not re-scramble the map here; change the FUNC params instead. Two numberings must be kept apart. (1) The ALLOCATOR's internal numbering, which is a property of CA_AIRFRAME and is UNCHANGED by any of this: CA_AIRFRAME 11 (tail servo) adds actuators motors-first, then servos, so it is always Motor1=101 main rotor [0,1], Servo1=201 yaw tail servo [-1,1], Servo2..Servo4=202..204 swash plate servos 1-3 [-1,1]. (2) The controls[] POSITIONS, which are freely chosen by PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>/HIL_ACT_FUNC<N> (function N lands on controls[N-1]) and are picked to reproduce ARDUPILOT'S OWN HELI CHANNEL ORDER, so that a RealFlight heli model already set up for ArduPilot flies here unchanged: controls[0..2]=swash plate servos 1-3 (functions 202/203/204), controls[3]=yaw tail servo (function 201), controls[7]=main rotor RSC (function 101). ArduPilot ground truth: AP_MotorsHeli_Swash.cpp:201-202 defaults swash servos 1-3 to SERVO1/2/3 (k_motor1/2/3 = 33/34/35); AP_MotorsHeli_Single.cpp:207 add_motor_num(CH_4) defaults yaw to SERVO4 (k_motor4 = 36); AP_MotorsHeli.h:34 '#define AP_MOTORS_HELI_RSC CH_8' defaults the rotor speed controller to SERVO8 (k_heli_rsc = 31). SIM_FlightAxis.cpp then ships output channel N as RealFlight channel N, so ArduPilot's RealFlight heli is rf0-2 swash, rf3 yaw, rf7 throttle - which is what this file reproduces.
ScaleComment: The "scale" column is the ONE thing here that is not free configuration, because it is a signal-domain conversion rather than a preference. PWMSim emits non-reversible Motor functions as [0,1] and EVERYTHING else as [-1,1] (PWMSim.cpp:77-88), while RealFlight wants [0,1] on every channel. So "unipolar" (pass through) is correct if and only if you assigned a Motor to that PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N>, and "bipolar" ((v+1)/2) is correct for servos, control surfaces and peripherals. Get it wrong and the failure is quiet: a motor read as bipolar idles at half throttle and never stops, and a surface read as unipolar loses its entire negative half and only ever deflects one way. RULE: change "scale" on a row whenever you change that channel's FUNC between a Motor and anything else. Here rf7 is the main rotor (Motor 1, unipolar, disarm 0.0) and everything else is a servo (bipolar).
ReverseComment: There is deliberately no "reverse": true anywhere in this file. Direction is not a property of the pipe, it belongs to whoever configures the aircraft, and there are two places to set it - both outside this file. (1) PX4 side: the PWM_MAIN_REV parameter is a bitmask over output channels, bit N-1 for channel N, and it IS honoured in SITL - it is applied in MixingOutput::output_limit_calc_single (mixer_module.cpp:531) which runs upstream of PWMSim, so it reaches RealFlight. Reverse channel 4 with 'param set PWM_MAIN_REV 8'; channel 9 is bit 8, i.e. 256. (Note PWM_MAIN_MIN/MAX are a different story - PWMSim overwrites them with setAllMinValues/setAllMaxValues, so per-channel endpoint trimming does NOT work in SITL.) (2) RealFlight side: each servo has its own direction in the model's servo setup. ON A HELI, REVERSING A SINGLE SWASH SERVO IS ALMOST ALWAYS WRONG - the three swash outputs are a coordinated triple, and flipping one of them alone tilts the swash the wrong way about one axis while leaving the others, which reads as roll/pitch cross-coupling rather than a clean reversal. Fix swash direction in the RealFlight model's servo setup, or with CA_SP0_ANG*, not by reversing one channel. The tail servo on ch4 is the one heli channel where a plain reversal is genuinely the right fix, and its symptom is unambiguous: the heli yaws away from the commanded direction and spins up rather than settling.
DemixComment: HeliDemix IS DEFERRED OFF ON PURPOSE and is not in Options below. MIXING MUST LIVE ON EXACTLY ONE SIDE OF THE LINK. PX4's allocator is already the mixer: CA_AIRFRAME 11 with CA_SP0_ANG 300/60/180 turns roll/pitch/collective into three swash servo positions, and this file ships those three positions to RealFlight UNTOUCHED - rf0/rf1/rf2 are literally Servo2/Servo3/Servo4 as the allocator computed them. So the RealFlight model must be a NON-MIXED, DIRECT-SERVO head: each RF channel wired straight through to the matching PWM channel, exactly the way a plane model is set up with ail/ele/thr/rud on ch1-4. If the model ALSO mixes, PX4's swash mix and the model's CCPM mix compose and roll and pitch cross-couple badly. That is the failure this default avoids. THIS ALSO MATCHES ARDUPILOT'S DEFAULT: SIM_FlightAxis.cpp:129-130 makes helidemix opt-in via a 'helidemix' substring in the frame name, so a plain ArduPilot RealFlight heli likewise sends raw swash positions with channel N = channel N. WHEN TO TURN IT BACK ON: only if your RealFlight model has a CCPM/eCCPM head whose mixing you cannot disable in the aircraft editor. Such a model wants roll/pitch/collective on rf0-2, not servo positions, so the bridge has to undo PX4's swash mix first - add "HeliDemix" back to Options (bitmask 9 becomes 13) and leave everything else alone. The option is fully supported and still lives in the bridge; it is merely not the right default for a directly-mapped model. WHAT IT DOES WHEN ENABLED: converts the three swash servo outputs on rf0-2 back into the roll/pitch/collective triple RealFlight expects: roll=(s1-s2)/1.732, pitch=((s1+s2)/2-s3)/1.5, col=(s1+s2+s3)/3, recentered to 0..1. The two divisors are NOT cosmetic - the raw differences carry gain 0.866 and 0.75 about the 0.5 centre where a full command needs 0.5, so without them roll is over-driven 1.732x and pitch 1.5x and RealFlight cyclic saturates at only +/-0.577 and +/-0.667 of command. Collective already has gain 0.5 and is left alone. The inverse is only exact for a swash geometry with servos 1 and 2 mirrored about the X axis and servo 3 on the axis, which is why the airframe overrides CA_SP0_ANG* to 300/60/180 instead of the PX4 defaults 0/140/220 - and the divisors follow from those angles, so changing one without the other breaks the decoupling. THE TWO DIVISORS ARE OURS, NOT ARDUPILOT'S. SIM_FlightAxis.cpp:348-350 divides by nothing at all - it is a plain unweighted inverse. That is exact only for a 140-degree head (AP_MotorsHeli_Swash.cpp:138-145 mixes H3_140 with flat +/-1.0 factors); on the 120-degree head both ArduPilot and this airframe actually use (AP_MotorsHeli_Swash.cpp:147-154 add_servo_angle -60/+60/180, the same geometry as our CA_SP0_ANG 300/60/180) the forward mixer picks up cos(30)=0.866 on roll against 0.5/1.0 on pitch, so ArduPilot's own round trip comes back with roll and pitch gains in the ratio 2/sqrt(3)=1.155 instead of 1. We divide that ratio out deliberately. Do not 'fix' this back to ArduPilot's version: it would restore an axis imbalance, not remove one.
DisarmComment: rf0-2 each carry an explicit "disarm" of 0.5, the neutral swash position, matching the explicit "disarm" every motor row already states. With HeliDemix now off by default these three rows are plain pass-through and nothing rewrites them after the fact, so hold-last would be safe here on that ground alone - but the values stay stated, because re-enabling HeliDemix for a CCPM model must not quietly reintroduce the hazard below, and get_FAbridge_params.py rejects a hold-last row on any slot an option rewrites. Omitting it would default to -1 = "hold last output". That is safe as the bridge stands - buildChannels() demixes out of a scratch copy and writes only out[], leaving the persistent channels[] untransformed, so a held slot holds a raw servo value and re-running the demix on it is idempotent. It was NOT safe before that split. HeliDemix used to be applied in place on channels[] every frame, so a held slot came back round already demixed and was demixed again: a non-involutive linear map that from a plausible in-flight swash (0.8, 0.3, 0.5) went chaotic and railed within ~3 frames at ~250 Hz. Neutral (0.5, 0.5, 0.5) is a fixed point, so pre-arm looked clean and only the armed->disarmed transition with a deflected swash tripped it - i.e. every normal landing - with nothing logged. get_FAbridge_params.py still rejects a hold-last row on any slot an option rewrites, and these rows still state their disarm value, because the property that makes hold-last safe lives in one function in another file and nothing here would notice if it were lost again. The same disarm path also runs if any mapped control arrives NaN while armed.
YawComment: rf3 is bipolar: under CA_AIRFRAME 11 the tail is a SERVO, so PWMSim normalises it to [-1,1] and 0.5 is zero tail pitch. That matches the collective-pitch tail rotor nearly every stock RealFlight single-rotor heli models. CA_AIRFRAME 10 would make the tail a motor clamped to [0,1], which clips every left-yaw command - measured against the bridge: yaw -3 rad/s produced rf3=0.000 and the tail idled on its lower clip, while type 11 gives 0.130 / 0.548 / 1.000 for -3 / neutral / +3.
AuxComment: rf4-6 and rf8-11 (RF ch5-7 and ch9-12) are mapped but carry no function in the shipped airframe: PWM_MAIN_FUNC5/6/7 are explicitly 0 and FUNC9 and up are unset, so PX4 emits 0.0 on those outputs and the bipolar conversion turns that into a steady 0.5 - neutral, and byte-identical to the UnmappedDefault these channels used to fall back on. That also keeps ArduPilot parity, where rf4-6 are unmapped too. They are listed anyway so that using one takes a parameter change and nothing else: set PWM_MAIN_FUNC<N> to Landing_Gear, a governor, a Servo, a Peripheral via Actuator Set, or anything else PX4 offers, and it appears on RealFlight channel N with no edit to this file. Remember the scale rule above if what you assign is a Motor.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: swash plate servo 1       <- controls[0]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC1 = 202, sent raw (ArduPilot SERVO1)
swash plate servo 2       <- controls[1]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC2 = 203, sent raw (ArduPilot SERVO2)
swash plate servo 3       <- controls[2]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC3 = 204, sent raw (ArduPilot SERVO3)
tail rotor pitch / yaw    <- controls[3]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC4 = 201 (ArduPilot SERVO4)
unassigned                <- controls[4]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC5 = 0, set it to use
unassigned                <- controls[5]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC6 = 0, set it to use
unassigned                <- controls[6]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC7 = 0, set it to use
unassigned                <- controls[7]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC8 = 0 (main rotor moved to ch9), set it to use
main rotor RSC / throttle <- controls[8]  PWM_MAIN_FUNC9 = 101 Motor 1 (moved here from ch8; not ArduPilot's SERVO8 default)
unassigned                <- controls[9]  set PWM_MAIN_FUNC10 to use
unassigned                <- controls[10] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC11 to use
unassigned                <- controls[11] set PWM_MAIN_FUNC12 to use
